verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN38 homolog, also called TGN46) is a type I transmembrane protein that cycles between the trans-Golgi network (TGN) and endosomes, with very transient passage through the plasma membrane as part of its recycling itinerary. Its dominant steady-state compartment is the TGN/Golgi; surface biotinylation studies show it is detected at the PM only transiently during clathrin-mediated endocytosis cycling, not as a stable surface resident. Checking contextual buckets: no documented cancer ecto-pool or cell-death-induced outer-leaflet display; no germline/developmental-restricted outer-leaflet display; not a lysosomal TM protein undergoing lysosomal exocytosis; the PM pool during recycling transit is transient and not a stable surface-accessible form; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering on the outer leaflet. Although TGN46 is technically a TM protein that transiently touches the PM, it is canonically classified as an endomembrane-resident cycling protein without a stable outer-leaflet-accessible pool.
predicted_key_uncertainty: TGN46 does cycle through the PM during TGN-endosome recycling; whether this transient PM transit constitutes a functionally accessible surface pool (dual_localization) is unresolved.
